title: vicia sativa subsp. cuneata
intro: Vicia sativa subsp. cuneata, also known as the narrow-leaved vetch, is a subspecies of the vetch plant, which is a type of legume native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Vicia sativa subsp. cuneata include its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
